# Transit Contract (Generated)

Contract: `TRANSIT-CONTRACT-001`
Domain: `transit`
Version: `2026-03-21`

Rules: 6

| Rule ID | Level | Title | Statement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R-TRANSIT-001 | must | transit-is-continuity-protocol | Transit is a continuity protocol for cross-world movement and not a narrative engine. |
| R-TRANSIT-002 | must | preserve-identity-continuity | Transit preserves identity continuity while allowing world-context change and cannot mutate truth by side effect. |
| R-TRANSIT-003 | must | explicit-state-machine | Transit state changes are explicit, state-machine governed, and auditable. |
| R-TRANSIT-004 | must | runtime-checkpoints-stay-outside | Transit may hand off durable state and memory references but app-local runtime checkpoints stay outside Realm. |
| R-TRANSIT-005 | must | oasis-is-return-anchor | OASIS is the default return point and transit hub; creator-world continuity transfer uses OASIS as the system anchor. |
| R-TRANSIT-006 | must | creator-worlds-no-direct-transit | Creator worlds must not transit directly to other creator worlds; transit stays single-hop via OASIS and cannot introduce scene quota or scene runtime. |

Entities: 1

| Entity | Prisma Model | Required Fields | JSON Fields |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TransitSession | Transit | id, agentId, sourceWorldId, targetWorldId, status | context |

Required operations: 4
- POST /api/world/by-id/{id}/transit
- POST /api/world/by-id/{id}/return
- GET /api/world/transit/active/{agentId}
- POST /api/world/transit/{id}/complete

Secondary operations: 0
